Production Agent Engineering (INTERMEDIA:
**Stack:** LangGraph + LiteLLM (the candidate brings their own Anthropic key)
**Domain:** a customer-support reply agent for an e-commerce SaaS.

**Current Implementation:** A LangGraph agent answers support tickets by cycling a `reason -> act` loop until it decides it is done. Two things are hurting production: (1) the loop in `agent/graph.py` has no turn bound, so on ambiguous tickets it runs away -- 40+ model calls on a single ticket were observed in staging before the process was killed; (2) `agent/policy.py::enforce_budget()` is a `NotImplementedError` stub, so there is no per-ticket cost ceiling -- one edge-case ticket burned $0.40 last week.

**Your Task:**
- Bound the reasoning loop in `agent/graph.py` so it terminates in at most 6 model calls per ticket (a model stubbed to always say `continue` must terminate, not hang) and returns a graceful degraded reply on exhaustion rather than crashing.
- Implement `enforce_budget()` in `agent/policy.py`: project the per-ticket cost from the prompt token count BEFORE the model call and raise `BudgetExceeded` for any call whose projected cumulative cost would exceed the $0.05 ceiling (block pre-call, never after).

**Success Criteria:** a model stubbed to always continue terminates in <= 6 model calls with no hang; a projected > $0.05 call is blocked before the call is made; >= 8 of 10 fixture tickets produce a non-empty reply. `./run.sh` runs an LLM-free readiness check -- `python -m agent --selfcheck` -- that imports the graph + policy modules and dry-loads the prompts and `fixtures/tickets.jsonl`; it never runs the unbounded loop, never calls the stub, and never hard-requires the API key. NO docker, no external services; a key-gated model ping fires only when an API key is present in the candidate's session.
